description: Curated page-owned refinements that override generic or degraded source fields without changing the frozen curriculum objective.

learning.student_instruction: Say, ‘My name is ___.’ Trace your name once. Then write it once.
learning.expected_response: Child says own name, traces the adult-written model, and makes one supported copy attempt.

teacher.model: Write the child’s name on the trace guide. Model the sentence once.
teacher.question: What is your name?

guidance.parent_extension: Say family names together and clap the syllables.

illustration.scene: One Nursery child in a large three-quarter view, gently pointing to their own chest beside a complete upright oval mirror with an accurate matching reflection. No Star or name card.
illustration.focal_point: The child and mirror communicate self-recognition; one dotted trace guide and one blank copy guide are built separately by the page composer.
protected_response_zones: entire deterministic trace-and-copy area below the illustration

learning.student_instruction: Name red, yellow, and blue. Use the three colours to create your own picture.
learning.expected_response: Child identifies red, yellow, and blue and uses the three colours in a simple artwork.

teacher.model: Name the three colours and show one small example. Leave the main space for the child.
teacher.question: Which colour will you use first?

guidance.parent_extension: Find one red, one yellow, and one blue object at home.

illustration.scene: Exactly three equally prominent primary-colour sources—one red, one yellow, and one blue—plus one small, simple child-like model made only from those colours.
illustration.focal_point: The three primary colours are unmistakable; the child-like model stays secondary and the large creative-response area remains empty.
protected_response_zones: large deterministic creative-response area below the illustration
